rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DHIREN DWIJEN DESAI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/7/24. (No earlier availability.) If the amended petition or amendment to the petition cannot be e-filed in time to give 15 days' notice after it is filed, Counsel should contact the examiner to request more time. [NB: The probate referee was appointed in the order appointing the administrator. The examiner's outgoing voice mail message does not prohibit a caller from leaving a voice mail message; it only warns that messages are not usually reviewed until the end of the day. The 10/24/23 hearing date was continued from the 9/26/23 hearing, which was a continuance from the 7/11/23 hearing date, so the attorney and any interested party should have been able to determine via the Court's website when the next hearing was coming up. The Victim's Information Guide attached to the attorney's Declaration filed on 12/7/23 does not identify the victim or the date of the occurrence, etc., so it is not supportive of the declaration.] Courtesy copies of the Corrected I&A and the fully amended petition (or the amendment to this petition) and proof of service of the amended petition (or amendment to the petition) must be received by the assigned Examiner by 1/29/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] All pleadings must show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FREDERICK HOSKING
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/13/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A petition for distribution must list and describe in detail the property to be distributed, including the complete legal description of real property. [CRC 7.651.] This must also be in the order. (2) A petition for extraordinary compensation must include, or be accompanied by, a statement of the facts upon which the petition is based. [CRC 7.702.] (Obtaining title reports does not appear to be extraordinary. Why was it necessary for the attorney to find an attorney for this administrator in an ancillary proceeding? Was this attorney not compensated in that ancillary proceeding? Attorney costs must be itemized, showing the date incurred, description, payee, and amount.) (3) Administrator requests to withhold a $35,000 reserve in paragraph 22 of this petition, but this is missing from the prayer. The prayer includes the statutory compensation to the administrator, which he waived in paragraph 18.a. (4) Paragraph 26 of the petition also shows the whole purported value of the estate. It would be clearer if cash were set forth separately and the calculation of the net distribution of cash to each heir were clearly shown. (5) Exhibit A shows the "inventory" value of the real property as $2,401,000, but the real property was appraised at $2,317,000. An amendment to the petition must be filed to address these issues. Notice must be given after the amendment is e-filed, unless the other party waives notice. Deadline is 2/2/24 for providing the assigned examiner a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 2-1/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.])

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ROBERT TERRY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Compliance Status Hearing (For The Conservator Of The Estate)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/13/24. (No earlier availability.) Notwithstanding the conservator of the person's suggestion that the Court accept his proof of mailing Notice of Conservatee's Rights filed on 8/9/23 as the "official" one, there were TWO orders of this Court filed on 8/28/23. Proof of mailing the Notice of Conservatee's Rights must be filed by the conservator of the ESTATE, showing the correct date of filing of the order appointing her. No Corrected I&A has been filed yet to remove the real properties if they are not conservatorship assets and to uncheck item 4 since a probate referee was appointed. Proof of mailing the Corrected I&A and notice of how to object to it must also be filed. Deadline is 2/2/24 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHIN HSIN LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/13/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The proof service filed on 12/6/23 shows that notice only of the petitioner's declaration was mailed on 11/17/23 (without the instructions for remote appearance attached). Notice of the 2/13/24 hearing of the AMENDED SPOUSAL PROPERTY PETITION must be served no later than 15 days before that date. (It might be helpful if the decedent's parents consented to this petition) Deadline for addressing the notice issue is 2/2/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT PON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WANDA MAY LOO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/23/2024. The following issues need to be cleared two weeks prior: (1) Petition #8 to identify the trustee of the devisee trust. (2) Petition #8 to state the husband's info (Bok Sing Loo). If deceased, to include the date of death. (3) Also, if the proposed executor is the trustee, all beneficiaries under the terms of the trust must be identified and noticed. Notice is on file; however, the examiner is unable to review as Petition does not identify the trustee/trust beneficiaries. (4) Need to attach a copy of the will to Petition. Prob. Code sec. 8002(b)(1). Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
